what_it_does: Defines a static React 'Calendar' screen mockup for the platform UI kit: a toolbar (filter/sort/search/clear/task-type), a 'Top Tasks & Reminders' row of eight task cards, a month navigator (June 2026), and a 7-column week grid with day cells holding colored task chips. Composed of small presentational components (TopTask, CalChip, Day, CalendarScreen) and exposed on window.CalendarScreen.
observations: Plain function components using JSX, no hooks, no state — a static layout.
Attached to the global via `window.CalendarScreen = CalendarScreen;` rather than ES module export.
All visual styling delegated to cv-cal-* / cv-btn-* classes (from platform.css); the file carries only structure and content.
Placeholder content throughout: 'Task Name', 'Due Date: 00/00/00', 'Lorem ipsum...'.
Color is passed as a string class name (color='purple', kind='yellow') matching the chip/task color variants in platform.css.
Inline SVG search icon with stroke=var(--fg-muted) tokens inlined in markup.
A few inline styles use var() tokens (e.g. font/color on the 'Manage' link and Task Type button).
Calendar weeks are hand-laid for a specific June 2026 layout (27-31 muted prev-month, day 15 marked today).
standouts: Uses the global-window registration pattern (window.CalendarScreen) instead of module export — consistent with a script-tag-loaded mockup kit.
Hardcoded calendar dates/layout for June 2026 — a fixed mockup, not a data-driven calendar.
conventions: Follows the kit's pattern of class-only styling (no raw CSS values in the component; even SVG strokes use tokens).
Decomposes into small reusable sub-components (TopTask/CalChip/Day) — good componentization for a mockup.
Global-window exposure breaks from ES-module conventions but is internally consistent for this kit.
concerns: Entirely static placeholder data — no props/state drive the calendar; it is a visual mockup, not functional.
List items rendered without stable keys in some maps (the headers map has keys; chips/days are positional).
notes: A static, placeholder-filled React mockup of a calendar screen styled purely via platform.css classes and registered on window.CalendarScreen.
questions: Is this intended to ever become data-driven, or is it permanently a visual mockup?
Where is window.CalendarScreen consumed (which host page mounts it)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the filename and content both present a calendar screen layout; it does exactly that as a static mockup.
intention_signals: Renders a representative calendar/task screen for the platform design kit so the design can be seen and judged, with structure cleanly separated from the shared stylesheet.
novelty: The screen is built as composable mockup primitives (Day/CalChip/TopTask) yet hardwired to one month — a mockup that is component-structured but content-frozen.
contribution: Brings a calendar/task-management screen layout to the platform kit — the structural template for filter toolbar, top-tasks row, month nav, and a chipped week grid.
summary_for_embedding: A static React calendar-screen mockup in the platform UI kit with a task toolbar, top-tasks row, June-2026 month grid, and colored task chips, styled via platform.css classes and exposed on window.CalendarScreen.
intention_for_embedding: To present a designed calendar/task screen for the platform kit as a viewable, class-styled mockup.
